channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: U.S.-Iran Tensions Escalate: Trade Deals Replace Hormuz Toll, Attacks on Ships Spark Global Concern
item.description: The article details the escalating conflict between the U.S. and Iran, focusing on President Donald Trump's shift from imposing a 20% toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz to pursuing tra...

item.title: Childcare worker faces 329 charges after alleged abuse of 158 children across Sydney
item.description: A parent whose children attended a childcare centre linked to accused paedophile Hamish Tait has described the situation as 'traumatic' following his arrest. Tait, 35, faces 329 charges of child abuse...

item.title: Edmonton Man Faces Assault Charges in Granville Street Incidents
item.description: Jiel Simon Uguak, 32, an Edmonton man arrested during the FIFA World Cup in Vancouver's Granville Street pedestrian zone, has had his bail hearing adjourned until next week. Uguak is accused of two co...

item.title: Costco Warns California Customers About Invasive Insect Threatening Wine Industry
item.description: Costco has issued a warning to California customers who purchased desert willow plants between June 24 and July 3, advising them to isolate suspect plants and contact county agricultural officials to ...

item.title: Trump Reverses Hormuz Shipping Fee Plan Amid Gulf Lobbying
item.description: President Donald Trump has reversed his controversial plan to impose a 20% fee on commercial tankers navigating the Strait of Hormuz, following intense lobbying from Gulf allies. The decision comes ju...

item.title: India Suspends Indus Waters Treaty Over Pakistan's Persistent Obstructionism
item.description: The Indus Waters Treaty, a landmark agreement between India and Pakistan, has been placed in abeyance due to persistent obstructionism by Pakistan, according to a detailed analysis. The article highli...

item.title: Ex-athletes shaping modern sports commentary and cultural narratives
item.description: The article explores the transition of retired athletes into sports commentary roles, highlighting their unique perspectives and influence on modern sports media. Former athletes like Gary Lineker and...

item.title: Former Police Minister Bheki Cele Testifies on Legal Obligations to Fund Apartheid Era Police Defence
item.description: Former police minister Bheki Cele testified before the Khampepe commission about his legal obligation to fund the defence of apartheid-era security police, despite personal reservations. Cele, who ser...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Trust Funds: Court Freezes Accounts of Separatist Lawyer
item.description: The personal and professional bank accounts of Jeff Rath, a leader in Alberta’s separatist movement, were temporarily frozen by an Alberta court after a dispute over millions of dollars in trust funds...

item.title: Governor Calls for UN Probe into Oyo School Abduction Controversy
item.description: Governor Seyi Makinde of Oyo State has sparked a political debate by calling for a United Nations investigation into the abduction of schoolchildren and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area. The i...

item.title: France vs Spain: High-Stakes World Cup Semifinal Preview
item.description: The FIFA World Cup semifinals on July 15, 2026, will see France face Spain in a highly anticipated clash on Bastille Day. Both teams are among the top-ranked sides globally, with France boasting Ousma...

item.title: Maria Matray: A Star of Weimar Cinema and Hollywood Choreographer
item.description: Maria Matray, born Maria Charlotte Stern in 1907, was a German-Jewish dancer, actress, and screenwriter who rose to fame in the 1920s and 1930s. She gained prominence as a star of late Weimar cinema, ...

item.title: Moreland Council Lists Liquidations for July 13

item.title: Former Manitoba MP Charged with Firearms Trafficking After Police Seize Over 400 Weapons
item.description: A former federal politician denies trafficking any firearms but acknowledges illegally transferring three guns after police seized 439 weapons from his western Manitoba home. Inky Mark, 78, was charge...

item.title: Study Links Sleep Deprivation to Increased Road Accident Risk in South Africa
item.description: A study by Discovery Insure has revealed that drivers with chronic sleep deprivation, or 'sleep debt', are significantly more likely to be involved in road accidents. The research analysed four years ...

item.title: Gordie Howe Bridge Opening Deal Sparks Concerns Over Transparency
item.description: The article details the ongoing negotiations for the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge, highlighting concerns from local officials about the lack of transparency in the agreement. Canadi...

item.title: NHS Delay in Treating Cancer-Stricken Grandmother Leads to Worsening Condition
item.description: Marie Stibbe, a 79-year-old grandmother from Devon, was diagnosed with liver cancer in March 2026. Despite experiencing severe symptoms, including itchy legs and diabetes, her NHS treatment was delaye...

item.title: Extreme Heat Threatens Europe's Nuclear Energy Security
item.description: France is reducing nuclear power output at up to five plants due to record-high temperatures that have raised river levels beyond safe cooling thresholds for reactors. This follows a deadly July heatw...
